title: mercurialis annua var. genuina
intro: Mercurialis annua var. genuina, also known as the annual mercury or dog's mercury, is a plant species that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as it contains compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a tincture or infusion, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
